free_text: -Optical-Model Analysis of P+208-Pb Elastic Scattering
From 15 - 1000 MeV.
W.T.H.O., H.H., N.E.D. And Experimental Site
For 20 - 50 MeV Data.
Gustaf Werner Institute, a.I., B.F., G.T. And
Experimental Site For 185 MeV Data.
Optical Model.
Optical Potential Parameters.
Data For Subent O00287002- O00287010 were Taken from
Main Reference.
Data For Subent O0287011-O0287017 were Received from
Nea-Db. These Data are a Part of B. Clark Collection.
N.O.
Some Corrections were Included by F. Chukreev
47.3 MeV are Added by CAJAD from Main Reference.
Last checking has been done.
Elastic Scattering Cross Section Divided by the
Rutherford Cross Section.
The Gustaf Werner Synchrocyclotron, in
Conjunction with a Magnetic Spectrometer.
.Protons from the Gustaf Werner Synchrocyclotron.
-En-Rsl-Fw- Monoenergetic 185 MeV Protons with an
Energy Spread of Less Than 200 KeV Fwhm.
The Scattered Protons were Momentum Analyzed in
A Magnetic Spectrometer with a Horizontal Opening
Angle of 1.17 Degrees Determined by a Collimator at
The Entrance.
For the Cross Section Measurements the Protons
Were Detected with a 16 Channel Scintillator Hodoscope
In the Polarization Measurements a Polarimeter with
Two Independent Channels was Used. The Total Energy
Resolution was in Both Cases Less Than 300 KeV Fwhm.
The Scattering Angle Could be Determined to Within
0.05 Degrees.
.An 80 Mg/Cm2 Thick Target of 208-Pb, Enriched to
99 Percent. P-P Scattering was Measured Using a Ch2
Target.
-By Authors- The Absolute Cross Sections were
Determined Relative to the P-P Scattering Cross
Section.
.Data Scanned from Figure 1C of Main Ref.
The Absolute Uncertainty in the Cross Sections
Was 8.3 %, Including the Errors in the Calibration
Procedure and the Target Thickness Determination.
N.O.
Reaction Line, Data and Common Section are
Corrected by F.E. Chukreev
Last checking has been done.
The Gustaf Werner Synchrocyclotron, in
Conjunction with a Magnetic Spectrometer.
.Protons from the Gustaf Werner Synchrocyclotron.
-En-Rsl-Fw- Monoenergetic 185 MeV Protons with an
Energy Spread of Less Than 200 KeV Fwhm.
The Scattered Protons were Momentum Analyzed in
A Magnetic Spectrometer with a Horizontal Opening
Angle of 1.17 Degrees Determined by a Collimator at
The Entrance.
For the Cross Section Measurements the Protons
Were Detected with a 16 Channel Scintillator Hodoscope
In the Polarization Measurements a Polarimeter with
Two Independent Channels was Used. The Total Energy
Resolution was in Both Cases Less Than 300 KeV Fwhm.
The Scattering Angle Could be Determined to Within
0.05 Degrees.
.An 80 Mg/Cm2 Thick Target of 208-Pb, Enriched to
99 Percent. P-P Scattering was Measured Using a Ch2
Target.
.Data Scanned from Figure 2B of Main Ref.
The Absolute
Polarization was Determined Relative to the
Polarization of Elastic Scattering from 12-C at 12.5
Degrees Lab.
The Error in the Absolute Scale of the
Polarization Data is 5 %.
The Uncertainty of Monitor Data.
N.O.
Reaction Line, Data and Common Section are
Corrected.
Last checking has been done.
En-Rsl-Fw at 50 MeV.
Targets Enriched to 99.3% in 208-Pb and with Nominal
Thicknesses of 5.0 and 10.0 Mgramm/Cm** were Used.
The Reaction Products were Observed
Simultaneously at Eight Angles Using an Array of Eight
Na-I(TL) Detector Assemblies Placed in a Crescent
Shaped Box Which Could be Rotated Around the Center 0F
A 117-Cm Inner Diameter Scattering Chamber.
By CAJAD.
Finite Geometry
Correction Factor.
Modification of
Willmes'S Method.
The First Correction Takes in Account That the
Measurements were done with a Finite Solid Angle, a
Beam of Finite Size at the Target, and a Beam Which in
First Approximation Converges Towards the Target.
The Second Correction is For the Fraction of
Elastically Scattered Protons Lost Due to the Nuclear
Reactions in the Scintillators.
Data Point Reader Uncertainty.
The Detector Box Contains Eight Interchangeable
Collimators with Apertures 10.00 Angular Degrees Apart.
The Accuracy with Which the Scattering Angles Could be
Set was +- 0.02 Angular Degree.
Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
Absolute Error of Measurements.
The Absolute Uncertainty Includes Contributions from
1. The Uncertainty of the Target Thickness,
2. The Uncertainty in the Beam Current Integration +-1%
3. The Absolute Error in the Solid Angle Determination,
4. The Uncertainty in the Direction of the Incident
Beam with the Respect to the Zero Degree Axisof the
Scattering Chamber - +-0.02 Degree,
5. The Uncertainty Im the Correction For Nuclear
Reactions in the Scintillators /+-20% of the
Correction/.
The Relative Uncertainties Include Contributions from
1. Counting Statistics,
2. Uncertainties in the Electronic Deadtime Correction
/+- 10% of the Correction/,
3. Uncertainties in the Setting of the Target Angle /+-
0.5 Degree/,
4. Uncertainties in the Setting of the Scattering Angle
/+- 0.02 Degree/,
5. The Relative Errors in the Solid Angle
Determination.
The Differential Cross Sections have Relative Errors
Which are in General Less Than 3%.
- By Compiler - The Density of Data Points on Figure is
Very High. Therefore Some Data Points Could be Omitted
At the Scanning. The Accuracy of Experimental Data has
Been Lost by Presentation Method.
